reasoning: The definition `g n := Real.sqrt (n + 1) - Real.sqrt n` exactly matches the informal $a_i = \sqrt{i+1} - \sqrt{i}$. The expression `∑ i ∈ range n, g i` corresponds to $\sum_{i<n} a_i$ since `Finset.range n = {0, 1, ..., n-1}`. The statement `Tendsto (fun n => ∑ i ∈ range n, g i) atTop atTop` is the standard Mathlib encoding of $\lim_{n \to \infty} \sum_{i<n} a_i = \infty$ for a real-valued sequence. The quantifier structure (over `n : ℕ`), the summation range, and the divergence-to-infinity conclusion all line up with the informal claim, so the formalization is logically equivalent.
